name: Jsonstore

unions.name: Json
unions.comment: The Json type combines both the Json Decoder and Encoder.

values.name: bool
values.comment:
import Json.Decode as D

    "true"
    |> (bool |> decode |> D.decodeString)
    --> Ok True

values.name: decode
values.comment: Returns the decoder of a Json type

values.name: decodeList
values.comment: Returns the decoder for a List of a Json type

values.name: delete
values.comment:
Deletes an Element.

Will be successfull even if the content is empty.

values.name: dict
values.comment:
import Json.Decode as D
    import Dict exposing (Dict)

    "{\"value\":42}"
    |> (int |> dict |> decode |> D.decodeString)
    --> Ok (Dict.singleton "value" 42)

values.name: encode
values.comment: Returns the encoder of a Json type

values.name: encodeList
values.comment: Returns the encoder for a List of a Json type

values.name: float
values.comment:
import Json.Decode as D

    "3.14" |> (float |> decode |> D.decodeString) --> Ok 3.14

values.name: get
values.comment:
Gets an Element

Returns `Nothing` if the element does not exist.
values.type: String.String -> Json.Decode.Decoder a -> Task.Task Http.Error (Maybe.Maybe a)

values.name: insert
values.comment:
Inserts a new Element.

Do not use this function to update fields, use update instead.

There is a max limit of 100kb that can be inserted at once.
Therefore never try to update a full list of object,
rather send an seperate update/delete file for every entry of the list.

values.name: int
values.comment:
import Json.Decode as D

    "1" |> (int |> decode |> D.decodeString) --> Ok 1

values.name: map
values.type: (a -> b) -> (b -> a) -> Jsonstore.Json a -> Jsonstore.Json b

values.name: object
values.comment:
import Json.Decode as D

    type alias Obj =
        { value : Int
        , name : String
        }

    "{\"value\":42,\"name\":\"Elm\"}"
    |> ( object Obj
        |> with "value" int .value
        |> with "name" string .name
        |> toJson
        |> decode
        |> D.decodeString
        )
    --> Ok {value=42,name="Elm"}

values.name: string
values.comment:
import Json.Decode as D

    "\"Hello World\""
    |> (string |> decode |> D.decodeString)
    --> Ok "Hello World"

values.name: update
values.comment:
First gets the value, then either inserts a new value or does nothing

Use delete if you want to delete an element.

There is a max limit of 100kb that can be inserted at once.
Therefore never try to update a full list of object,
rather send an seperate update/delete file for every entry of the list.
values.type: String.String -> Jsonstore.Json a -> (a -> a) -> Task.Task Http.Error ()

values.name: with
values.type: String.String -> Jsonstore.Json a -> (obj -> a) -> Jsonstore.JsonObject (a -> fun) obj -> Jsonstore.JsonObject fun obj

values.name: withList
values.type: String.String -> Jsonstore.Json a -> (obj -> List.List a) -> Jsonstore.JsonObject (List.List a -> fun) obj -> Jsonstore.JsonObject fun obj

values.name: withMaybe
values.type: String.String -> Jsonstore.Json a -> (obj -> Maybe.Maybe a) -> Jsonstore.JsonObject (Maybe.Maybe a -> fun) obj -> Jsonstore.JsonObject fun obj
